from qiskit.circuit.library import CXGate

# Create a simple 2-qubit circuit with some gates to optimize/cancel
qc = QuantumCircuit(2)
qc.h(0)
qc.cx(0, 1)
qc.x(0)  # Will be optimized/simplified

pm.append(do_while(lambda _: True, InverseCancellation([CXGate()])))  # CX cancellation loop

# Run the transpilation
optimized_qc = pm.run(qc)

print("Original circuit depth:", qc.depth())
print("Optimized circuit depth:", optimized_qc.depth())
```

4. Appends an InverseCancellation pass configured for CXGate with a do_while loop instead of deprecated max_iteration
5. Runs the PassManager on the circuit and prints depth before/after to show optimization

Note: The do_while loop will repeat the InverseCancellation pass until no more cancellations are possible, which is functionally similar to the old max_iteration=3 behavior but uses the modern API pattern.
